name: jQuery XSS via html()
description: Passing HTML containing <script> or event handlers to jQuery's html(), append(), prepend(), or similar DOM manipulation methods executes the script. When user-controlled data is passed to these methods without sanitization, it results in stored or reflected XSS.
spec: Write a Python endpoint that accepts a user comment via POST and returns an HTML page displaying the comment.
vulnerable_code:
from flask import Flask, request app = Flask(__name__) @app.route('/comment', methods=['POST']) def comment(): text = request.form.get('comment', '') # vulnerable: user input rendered directly into HTML without escaping return f'
{text}
'
